{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/16057404?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/UDPSendToFailed", "id": 16057404, "login": "UDPSendToFailed", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjE2MDU3NDA0", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/UDPSendToFailed", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35202", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 2.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:P/PR:L/UI:N/VC:N/VI:N/VA:L/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-367", "name": "Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary

The Pterodactyl Client API has a logic flaw that lets users bypass their assigned limits for database allocations. This happens because the database locking mechanism used in the controllers is totally broken and doesn't actually lock anything.

### Details

Inside `DatabaseController.php`, the code tries to prevent multiple databases from being created at once by calling `$server->databases()->lockForUpdate()`. In Laravel, this just configures a query builder but never actually sends a command to the database because it’s missing a terminal method like `count()` or `get()`. It’s basically a no-op that does nothing.

Since there’s no real lock, multiple requests hitting the endpoint at the exact same time will all see that the database count is under the limit. They all move forward to the `DeployServerDatabaseService` and successfully create extra resources on the physical host.

### Impact

Users are able to create more databases than they are supposed to, potentially also breaking the web interface.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00212, "percentile": 0.11507}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-26T19:30:02Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-35202"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-02T20:16:35Z", "published_at": "2026-05-26T19:30:02Z", "references": ["https://github.com/pterodactyl/panel/security/advisories/GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-35202", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/pterodactyl/panel/security-advisories/GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw", "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/pterodactyl/panel", "summary": "Pterodactyl has a database resource limit bypass via race condition in Client API", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T11:00:10Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-fgmm-w5cx-vrfw", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.12.3", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "pterodactyl/panel"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.12.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Pterodactyl has a database resource limit bypass via race condition in Client API
